body.title: Residue scan — 'anything else interesting?' after reflex reply
body.result: residue_scan.scan_after_reply body replaces T-non-terminal-emission stub. Chunks input via T-input-chunker, classifies first chunk as reflex, scores residue via graph-tree signals (question-mark / content-words / density / habit-match), spawns continuation_reply pursuit above threshold. IGOR_RESIDUE_SCAN_ENABLED gate default false (log-only rollout). Must-not-raise contract preserved. 15 tests. Commit be90b5d4.
body.description:
After a reflex reply handles part of input (greeting, ack, farewell), the rest of the input may contain substantive content the reflex didn't address. Need a cheap salience scan that runs in the resumed parent pursuit (per T-non-terminal-emission) and decides: is there more here worth responding to? Uses graph-tree lookups (habit_list, hot_attractors, NE-predict) to classify residue — not LLM. If high-salience content remains, spawn a continuation-reply pursuit. If not, terminate silently.

**Affected files:** likely new `wild_igor/igor/cognition/residue_scan.py`; callsite in the resumed parent pursuit (from T-non-terminal-emission); `tests/test_residue_scan.py`.
**Design rules:** docs-in-code; test-plan-or-why-not (covered); oop-first (class or function — scan is mostly functional, state is the input); no-new-memory-schemas.
**Scope boundary:** IN: residue(full_input, addressed_span) → {salience_score, residue_content, suggested_handler}; graph-tree classification (reuse habit_list / hot_attractors / NE-predict); salience-threshold gate (below → silent terminate). OUT: continuation-reply articulation (that's the normal reply path, just invoked on residue); register coherence (follow-on if needed).
**Test plan:** input="hi, and can you explain X?" with addressed="hi" → residue contains 'can you explain X?' with high salience; input="thanks!" with addressed="thanks!" → residue empty, silent terminate; input="hi, btw, random phrase with no clear intent" → low-confidence residue, silent terminate (don't chase noise); salience threshold tunable via env var.
